notes.anchor: in distinction he yields to no Roman knight
notes.note: An eques Romanus belonged to the equestrian order, the propertied non-senatorial class from which businessmen and tax-farmers were drawn; "distinction" (splendor) is the standing such a man held within that order.

notes.anchor: His affairs in Africa I commend to you
notes.note: Aufidius, one of Q. Turius's heirs (Fam. 12.26), gets a second, dedicated letter of recommendation to Cornificius the proconsul concerning his own business interests in the province of Africa.
